title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens has not yet signed a pro contract with the Boston Bruins after finishing his sophomore season at Boston College.
Hagens, a top-10 pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, recorded 47 points in 34 games during the 2025-26 season.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm is open to adding prospects like Hagens to the lineup if they are ready to compete.

title: ESPN reporter explains why June 1st could be a big day for teams looking to trade for A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A trade for A.J. Brown may be more favorable post-June 1 due to reduced dead money on Eagles’ salary cap.
The Eagles would incur $43.5 million in dead money if Brown is traded before June 1, but $16.353 million after.
The Patriots are considered a top suitor for Brown, with trade discussions possibly involving a first-round pick exchange.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her mother’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Allyson Bogdanski, from Medford, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon to honor her mother's battle with breast cancer.
Her mother, a two-time breast cancer survivor, received treatment at Dana-Farber, inspiring Bogdanski's participation in the marathon.
Bogdanski views the marathon as a tribute to her mother's courage and the support from Dana-Farber's care team.

title: GoFundMe for Boston officer charged in fatal shooting soars past $400K
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O'Malley is charged with manslaughter in the shooting death of carjacking suspect Stephenson King.
A GoFundMe campaign for Officer O'Malley's family has raised over $407,000 for essential expenses after his suspension.
O'Malley is on paid administrative leave following his suspension by the Massachusetts Peace Officer Standards and Training Commission.

title: AI attack ads are hitting Massachusetts political campaigns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are considering legislation to regulate AI in political ads to prevent misleading content in campaigns.
Republican state representative Marc Lombardo used AI-generated content in an attack ad against opponent Daniel Darris-O’Connor.
Proposed bills H.5093 and H.5094 aim to regulate AI use in political advertising, focusing on election misinformation and disclosure requirements.

title: GoFundMe launched for Mass. family after teen dies in crash from overpass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travis Teixeira, 18, died after his car fell from a Route 2 overpass in Leominster, Massachusetts, police report.
A GoFundMe campaign raised over $70,000 to support Teixeira’s family with funeral costs and financial needs.
Teixeira was described as a smart, passionate young man accepted into every college he applied to.

title: ‘Stay strong’: David Ortiz’s defiant declaration in the wake of tragedy still resonates with Boston
author: Chad Finn
bullets: David Ortiz addressed Fenway Park five days after the Boston Marathon bombings on April 20, 2013.
Ortiz thanked city officials and declared, 'This is our [expletive] city, and nobody is gonna dictate our freedom.'
Ortiz's message resonated with Boston as a symbol of resilience and strength during a somber time.

title: Holy Cross student missing after canoe capsizes in Connecticut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dominick Tucci, a Holy Cross student, is missing after a canoe capsized on Lake Hayward, East Haddam, Connecticut.
The Connecticut Environmental Conservation Police said search teams used watercraft, divers, and aerial surveillance over the weekend.
Holy Cross is offering counseling services to students and remains in contact with law enforcement as search efforts continue.

title: The unsolved murder of Kendric Price, a beloved youth mentor gunned down on his childhood street in Dorchester
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Kendric Price, a youth mentor and basketball coach, was murdered on Greenwood Street in Dorchester in March 2019.
Despite ongoing investigations, no arrests have been made in Price's shooting death, and witnesses haven't come forward.
Price's mother, Carol, continues his community work through Big Business Network and urges witnesses to provide information.

title: The basement of this $4.7m Lynnfield home known for its holiday decor is like a private club
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: 1 Haywood Farm in Lynnfield is listed for $4.695 million and features a basement designed like a private club.
The 8,190-square-foot home includes an eight-person bar, private screening room, and a spa-grade sauna.
Known for elaborate holiday decorations, the property attracts thousands of visitors during Halloween and Christmas.
